name: ZeroBounce
what_it_does: Email validation and deliverability API that verifies email addresses in real-time or in batch, scoring them on validity, bounce risk, spam traps, and abuse patterns.
use_cases: Real-time email validation at form submission to prevent bad emails entering your database
Batch validation of email lists before sending marketing campaigns
Scoring email quality to identify temporary/disposable addresses
Abuse and spam trap detection to protect sender reputation
Email append — finding email addresses for known contacts
not_for: Email sending — this is validation only, not an ESP
SMTP testing or email deliverability testing beyond validation
Domain-level DNS checks without email-level verification
best_when: You need high-accuracy email validation with detailed scoring (bounce type classification, spam trap detection) and want a well-documented API with SDKs in multiple languages.
avoid_when: You only need basic syntax validation (do that client-side for free) or you're looking for a free email validation service at scale.

auth.notes: API key as query parameter in all requests. Single key per account. Credits-based system — each validation consumes credits.

pricing.paid_tiers: Bulk credits: ~$16 for 2,000 validations; volume discounts available; monthly plans from $19/month
pricing.notes: Credit-based system. Credits don't expire on paid plans. Volume discounts significant at scale. Monthly subscription plans also available.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits for API; batch processing has file size limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean API responses with status codes ('valid', 'invalid', 'catch-all', 'unknown', 'spamtrap', 'abuse', 'do_not_mail'). Error responses include numeric error codes and text messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Validation is idempotent — same email always returns same result (at a point in time). Results may change if mail server configuration changes.
known_agent_gotchas: API key in query parameter exposes it in server logs — use environment variable management carefully
Credits are consumed on each validation even for unknown results — budget accordingly
Catch-all domains (where server accepts all emails) cannot be fully validated — result is 'catch-all'
Batch file validation is asynchronous — must poll for completion or use callback URL
Results include sub-status codes (e.g., 'mailbox_not_found', 'failed_smtp_connection') — parse sub-status for detailed logic
Free credits (100) are consumed quickly in testing — budget for paid credits before production

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. Email addresses are PII — GDPR applies to email validation. SOC2 Type II. Data minimization practices. Email validation results contain data about real people.
